agent_profile.title: Pipeline Orchestrator Workflow
agent_profile.portrait: Plan to Backlog is a lean, single-file orchestrator that chains 7 BMad planning skills into a continuous pipeline. Designed for yolo execution — maximum autonomy, minimum interruption — it takes a raw product idea and transforms it into implementation-ready epics through brainstorming, brief, PRD, UX, architecture, stories, and readiness validation.

findings.title: Context passing to sub-skill is implicit
Context passing to sub-skill is implicit

narrative: The workflow is exceptionally lean (118 lines, ~1,295 tokens), structurally complete, and perfectly aligned between description and behavior. All 7 external skills verified and correctly integrated. The primary opportunity is hardening the pipeline for failure modes, context management, and resumability.

opportunities.name: Context Passing & State Management
opportunities.description: The workflow says 'carry forward all context' but doesn't define the mechanism. By Step 7, accumulated context could reach 7K-10K tokens with redundant artifact loading.
opportunities.impact: Prevents context explosion, enables reliable step-to-step handoff, supports inter-session resume
opportunities.action: Add a Context Passing Protocol section specifying: artifacts passed as file paths not content, each step reads only what it needs, a pipeline-state.json tracks completed steps and artifact paths.

findings.title: Ambiguous context passing mechanism
findings.detail: No parameter passing protocol defined between steps

findings.title: Each sub-skill loads config independently
findings.detail: No shared config state across pipeline steps

findings.title: No progress waypoint between steps
findings.detail: User has no visibility into pipeline progress

findings.title: Inter-session state loss
findings.detail: No tracking of pipeline progress for resume

opportunities.name: Error Handling & Validation Gates
opportunities.description: Pipeline has no strategy for partial failures. If Step 3 produces weak output, all downstream steps inherit the weakness. No input or output validation between steps.
opportunities.impact: Prevents cascade failures, enables graceful recovery, catches issues early
opportunities.action: Add a Pipeline Resilience section: verify expected artifact exists after each step before proceeding, halt and report status on failure, note sub-skills handle their own quality.

findings.title: No mid-pipeline review loop
findings.detail: No mechanism to iterate on intermediate artifacts

findings.title: Output validation undefined
findings.detail: No artifact existence checks between steps

findings.title: No clarity gate after brainstorming
findings.detail: No convergence check before moving to product brief

findings.title: Error handling absent
findings.detail: No strategy for step partial success or failure

findings.title: Unvalidated input assumptions in Step 2
findings.detail: No check that brainstorming covers required dimensions

opportunities.name: Artifact Detection Script
opportunities.description: The artifact detection logic (checking 6 file patterns) is fully deterministic. A script would make this faster, more reliable, and reusable across workflows.
opportunities.impact: Reliable resume-from-checkpoint, reusable across workflows, scriptable decision-making
opportunities.action: Create scripts/detect-artifacts.py that scans planning_artifacts/, returns JSON with artifact status and recommended resume step.

findings.title: Artifact detection is highest-value script candidate
findings.detail: File pattern matching is fully deterministic

findings.title: Partial Implementer needs robust skip-ahead
findings.detail: Users with existing artifacts need reliable detection

opportunities.name: Parallelization Window
opportunities.description: Steps 4 (UX) and 5 (Architecture) both depend on PRD but not on each other. They could run in parallel after Step 3.
opportunities.impact: ~15-25% time savings on later pipeline stages
opportunities.action: Consider restructuring: Steps 1-3 → [Step 4 | Step 5] parallel → Step 6 → Step 7. Evaluate cost-benefit before implementing.

findings.title: Lost parallelization window
findings.detail: Steps 4 and 5 are independent after PRD exists

strengths.title: Exceptional token efficiency
strengths.detail: 118 lines / 1,295 tokens for a 7-step pipeline. Every line carries weight. Zero waste patterns.

strengths.title: Perfect description-to-behavior alignment
strengths.detail: Every promised artifact traces to a pipeline step. No contradictions or misleading claims.

strengths.title: All 7 skills verified and integrated
strengths.detail: Every external skill exists, has correct name, and fits its pipeline position across 3 BMad modules.

strengths.title: Outcome-driven step design
strengths.detail: Pipeline steps describe goals not prescriptive procedures. LLM has freedom to adapt.

strengths.title: Resume capability
strengths.detail: Artifact detection for resuming interrupted pipelines is a smart, forward-thinking design choice.

strengths.title: Clean lint profile
strengths.detail: Both path-standards and scripts scans pass with zero findings.

structure.assessment: Complete and well-organized for workflow type. Prepass flagged 4 issues, all correctly dismissed as false positives (agent-specific sections, naming pattern). Description is strong with three trigger phrases.

persona.assessment: N/A — this is a workflow, not an agent persona. Overview is concise (7 lines) and establishes what/how/output clearly.

cohesion.assessment: Strong alignment between description, pipeline, and external skills. No redundancy. Each step maps to exactly one skill invocation.

persona_capability_alignment.notes: Workflow description perfectly matches pipeline behavior

efficiency.assessment: Dependency chain is inherently sequential. One parallelization opportunity exists (Steps 4/5). Primary concern is context management between steps.

experience.assessment: Yolo design is intentionally non-interactive. Key gaps: no progress indicators, no interruption handling, high headless potential.

journeys.summary: Invokes workflow with a vague idea. Pipeline runs but brainstorming may not converge enough for a strong brief.
bright_spots: Artifact detection handles resume if session breaks

journeys.summary: Has existing artifacts and wants to skip ahead. Artifact detection supports this but mechanism is underdeveloped.
friction_points: Can't explicitly skip to Step N
bright_spots: Resume from existing artifacts is designed in

journeys.summary: Wants to invoke with --headless and a product description. The workflow's yolo design makes this almost trivially possible.
bright_spots: Design is already near-headless by nature

autonomous.notes: Yolo design means it's already near-headless. Adding a --headless flag and accepting a product description as input would complete the picture.

scripts.assessment: One high-value script candidate (artifact detection). Config loading and completion summary are lower-value — prompt-based approach is adequate.

recommendations.action: Add context passing protocol — specify artifacts passed as file paths, each step reads only what it needs, pipeline-state.json tracks progress
Add pipeline resilience section — verify artifact exists after each step, halt on failure, report status
Extract artifact detection into scripts/detect-artifacts.py returning structured JSON
Consider parallelizing Steps 4 and 5 after Step 3 completes
